titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Trevor Winkfield Papers
titlestmt.author: by Jessica Tai

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday February 28, 2020 at 04:02

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

langmaterial.language: Material is primarily in English with some material in French and Spanish.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: January 2011 Acquisition: purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Alfred Z. Baker, Jr. Fund, 2011.
May 2017 Acquisition: purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Alfred Z. Baker, Jr. Fund, 2017.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Trevor Winkfield Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Trevor Winkfield Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

Information included in the Content Description note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.

bioghist.head: Trevor Winkfield
bioghist.p: Trevor Winkfield (born Leeds, England, 1944) is a British-born artist and writer. Winkfield graduated from Leeds College of Art in 1964 and earned an M.A. in Fine Art from the Royal College of Art in London in 1967. Winkfield has lived in New York since 1969. He has collaborated on book cover designs, poetry journals and other writing and art projects with poets and writers, including John Ashbery, Harry Mathews, Charles North, Ron Padgett, James Schuyler, and John Yau. Winkfield's collaborative work included a poetry journal titled Juilliard , that was published from 1968-1972.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains correspondence, subject files, writings, artwork, and publication material by or relating to the artist and writer Trevor Winkfield. Included is complete issues, as well as source material for Winkfield's poetry journal, Juilliard , which he published from 1968-1972. Also included is writing works by Harry Matthews, Charles North, Ron Padgett, James Schuyler, John Yau, and others. Artwork included in the collection includes paintings and collages for book projects that Winkfield collaborated on with other writers and artists. Artwork consists of collage mock ups as well as paintings for select book projects.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two groupings: January 2011 Acquisition, 1928 - 2009; and May 2017 Acquisition, 1983 - 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged into four series: Correspondence, Juillard , Writing, and Subject files and artwork.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Alfred Z. Baker, Jr. Fund, 2011.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This acquisition contains correspondence, writings, subject files, artwork, source material, as well as complete copies of Juilliard .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged alphabetically by correspondent's last name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes correspondence with poets, writers, and artists, including those whom Winkfield collaborated on poetry publication and art projects with, such as Larry Fagin, Peter Gizzi, Harry Matthews, Charles North, Ron Padgett, James Schuyler, and John Yau.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a photograph of Fairchild from 1956.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains outgoing correspondence to Z Press, Inc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material includes source material for Juillard magazine, including writings, correspondence, photographs, and poems. Juillard was a poetry journal published by Winkfield in England between 1968 and 1972.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged chronologically, with complete issues of Julliard arranged at the beginning of the series.

did.unittitle: Source material for the Juillard Spring 1968 Issue
Johns, Jasper: "High School Days" photographic print
Schneeman, George & Ron Padgett: "What the Midnight Moonshine Means to Me"
Layout featuring photographs of artwork by Saul Steinberg and George Inness
Layout featuring photographs of artwork by M.C. Escher and Rene Magritte
Source material for the Juillard Spring 1968 Issue Beach Supplement
Breton, Andre: "A Dream" translated by Ron Padgett
Oppenheim, Meret: typed poems translated by Marguerite Edmonds
Source material for the Juillard Winter 1968 Issue supplement
Oppenheim, Meret: poems translated by Michael Bullock
Source material for the Juillard Winter 1968-1969 Issue
Burckhardt, Rudy: "The Strange Case of the Disappearance of Professor Arbuthonot" photographic prints
Johns, Jasper: "Subway" photo by Rudy Burckhardt
Winkfield, Trevor: "When 'It's The Idea That Counts' Isn't Enough"
Source material for the Juillard Winter 1968-1969 Issue Superrealism Pinecone supplement
Disch, Thomas: "The Turtle's Dream" & other poems
Giorno, John: "From the Kamrma Sutra of John Griorno"
Hammond, Paul: "Notes and Jottings: About Automata"
Hughes, Patrick: "Partial Anthology of Contradictory Objects"
Jammes, Frances: "The Little Negress" translated by Paul Hammond
Jung, R.G.: "Intro to Tom Veitch's 'The Louis Armed Story' "
MacAdams, Lewis: "The Campaign Trail Part 2" & "Quicksand Mountain Breakdown"
Picabia, Francis: "Aphorisms" and "Life Can Be So Complicated: A Serial" translated by Paul Hammond
Schuyler, James: "Jelly Jelly," "Things to Do," and "Dreams"
Source material for the Juillard Fall 1970 Issue
Baxter, Glen: "Up in the Rocks," "The Bang," "What to Do," "ZZZ at the Winter Palace," "The Polo Light," "The Luminous Hours," and "Around the World"
Cendrars, Blaise: "Islands" translated by Ron Padgett
Mallarme, Stephanie: "Its Pure Nails" translated by Joachim Neugroschel
Pleynet, Marcelin: "4 Poems" translated by Bill Zavatsky
Tzara, Tristan: "The Seaman," "The Dada Review," "Cinema Calendar of the Abstract Heart,"' "The Dwarf in his Cornet," and "Retreat" translated by Lee Harwood
Source material for the Juillard Summer 1970 Issue
Coolidge, Clark and Larry Fagin: Poem
Dicabia, Francis: "A Myopic's Hospitality" translated by Paul Hammond
Duchamp, Marcel: "Postcard" translated by Paul Hammond
Elmslie, Kenward: "My Holy City Geography Book" from "The Orchid Stones"
Fagin, Larry: "A Wild Night in Old Chinatown"
Fun With Words Writer's Workshop poems
Hammond, Paul: "Notes and Jottings: About Sculptors and Their Sculpture"
Klein, Yves: "A Broad Outline of the Economic System of 'La Revolution Bleue' " translated by Paul Hammond
Leon: "The Sweetie Pie Pharmacist" and "Wives' Tricks"
Lespes, Paul: "The Isidore Ducasse I Knew" translated by Paul Hammond
Melies, Georges: correspondence translated by Paul Hammond (photocopy)
Morgenstern, Christian: "The White Picket Fence" translated by David Lehman
Peterson, Chris: "Nonpareil Cookie 5 Years Ago," "To Grandma's House," "Three Honest Animals," "Frogs," and "A Sunny Day"
Towle, Tony: "Second Pythian" and "Romance, on the Evils of Drink"
An activity situation using Hannah Weiner's "Open House"
Source material for the Juillard Winter 1970-1971 Issue
Badin, Georges: two poems translated by Joachim Neugroschel
Benn, Gottfried: "Beautiful Youth" translated by Joachim Neugroschel
du Bouchet, Andre: selected poems translated by Paul Auster
Fagin, Larry: "Narrative Technique #1," Narrative Technique #2," and "Narrative Technique #5 (After Conrad)"
Feneon, Felix: "Moral Tales" translated by Paul Hammond
Jarry, Alfred: "The Photography of Accidents" translated by Bill Zavatsky
Reverdy, Pierre: "Movie," "Wind and Spirit," "Fetish," and "Tenderness" translated by Bill Zavatsky
Rouseu, Raymond: "The Star on the Forehead" and "Among the Blacks" translated by Paul Hammond
Soupault, Philippe: "Sport Items" translated by Joachim Neugroschel
Trakl, George: "Childhood" translated by Joachim Neugroschel
Source material for the Juillard Spring 1972 Issue
Allais, Alphonse: "The Little Linen Marker" translated by Paul Hammond
Apollinaire, Guillaume: "The Moon King" translated by Ron Padgett
Bachelard, Baston: "Autreamont: Poet of the Muscles and the Cry" translated by Bill Zavatsky
Baxter, Glen: "The Roulette Saloon," "Coppices," "In the Ledge at Tetrian," "Bothy Weekend," "Campus Rivals," and "Bunker Retreat"
Cravan, Arthur: "Elephant Languor" translated by John Ashbury
Hughes, Patrick: "Two Projects for a Railway Line"
Koethe, John: "Seascape with Clouds and Birds"
Ratcliff, Carter: "Jack Frost Paints the Trees" and "Operative"
Soupault, Philippe: Poems translated by Larry Fagin and Daird Ball
Zavatsky, Bill: "Asia," "Spring in Fever," "Generative," "After Ice," and "A Preconscious Autobiography"
de Maria, Walter: drawing and photo
Fagin, Larry: "Fragment" & "Under the Volcano" (with Michael Brownstein)
Hammond, Paul: "At Home with Jules Verne"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published by Juilliard Editions. Contains pen and ink drawing by Joe Brainard of original cover art.

did.unittitle: Ratcliff, Carter: "Frost Flavers" and "The Arms of Morpheus"

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged alphabetically by author's last name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains writings by other poets and writers. Also included is translated works.

did.unittitle: Crase, Douglas: "Amerifil.Ext Out-takes for Myself and Friends"
Mathews, Harry: "The Bad Dreams of Carlos Ludovicus Dodgson"
Mathews, Harry: "Memorial: A Record of Solid and Liquid Foods Absorbed in the Course of the Year"
Mathews, Harry: "Miss Unity's Book of Reminders"
Mathews, Harry: "The Pursuit of the Whole"
North, Charles: poems and other writings
Reverdy, Pierre: "Prose Poems" translated by Ron Padgett
Schuyler, James: "The Home Book" galley proofs

Schuyler, James: "The Home Book" poems with Robert Dash
Schuyler, James: "The Home Book" printed material
Schuyler, James: "The Home Book" Berrigan letter
Schuyler, James: "The Home Book" various photocopied writings and poems

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged alphabetically by subject's or artist's last name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains subject files relating to artists, writers, and poets. Material includes articles, clippings, drawings, and other ephemera.

did.unittitle: Elmslie, Kenward: Chelsea Hotel Project with Ron Padgett
Finlay, Ian Hamilton: articles and clippings
Williams, Jonathan: "A Small Exhibition of Jargon Books and Books by Jonathan Williams"

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged alphabetically by author's last name.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Alfred Z. Baker, Jr. Fund, 2017.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This acquisition contains artwork for collaborative projects that Winkfield produced with writers and poets. Material consists of paintings, collages, correspondence, drawings, notes, mockups, and trial pieces for and relating to book projects.

did.unittitle: Ashbery, John and Trevor Winkfield: Faster Than Birds Can Fly collage mock ups
Ashbery, John and Trevor Winkfield: Faster Than Birds Can Fly notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains "Elements" notes. Also contains a holograph of the poem "Faster Than Birds Can Fly," with notations by Trevor Winkfield.

did.unittitle: Ashbery, John and Trevor Winkfield: Faster Than Birds Can Fly paintings
Ashbery, John and Trevor Winkfield: Novel collages
Fagin, Larry and Trevor Winkfield: Dig & Delve collages
Gizzi, Peter and Trevor Winkfield: Periplum: Or, I the Blaze painting

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a study in paint, for the front cover.

did.unittitle: North, Charles and Trevor Winkfield: Elevenses collage trial pieces
North, Charles and Trevor Winkfield: Elevenses correspondence
North, Charles and Trevor Winkfield: Elevenses collages
North, Charles and Trevor Winkfield: Elevenses paintings
North, Charles and Trevor Winkfield: New and Selected Poems painting

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a painting for the cover.

did.unittitle: North, Charles and Trevor Winkfield: Tulips artwork and collages

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains four collage drawings, a study for the front cover, a drawing for the rear cover, and a vignette drawing.

did.unittitle: North, Charles and Trevor Winkfield: What It Is Like: New and Selected Poems painting

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a painting for the front and back covers.

did.unittitle: Padgett, Ron and Trevor Winkfield: Do The Math artwork and collages
Padgett, Ron and Trevor Winkfield: How To Be A Woodpecker collages
